label: The Sorted Shelf
subject: Binary search (books on a shelf)
bossHook: Sixteen books shelved by call number, and a reader waiting. Four checks to find the right spine — split the shelf in half each time.

vocab.hint: Pull the MIDDLE book — its number tells you which half to search — then split what's left again.

first-cut.title: Start in the Middle
first-cut.hook: Eight books, shelved in order. A beginner starts at book one. A librarian pulls the MIDDLE book — its number says which way to go.
first-cut.winText: Middle first, then the middle of the half that was left. Sorted shelves are made for this.

learn.frame: The shelf is sorted end to end, but you've only got a few checks — which book do you pull FIRST?

reveal.body: Pulling the MIDDLE book let its call number point you at the right half — so one check ruled out the other half of the shelf. Start at the middle: it's the habit the whole method rests on.
reveal.inTheWild: a paper dictionary — nobody starts at page one, you flip to the middle and split from there.

halving.title: Halve the Shelf
halving.hook: Twelve books. Each check should discard HALF the shelf — not slide over one spine at a time.
halving.winText: Every check tossed half the shelf. Twelve books down to one in a few pulls — that's the payoff of sorted order.

failText.not-halving: You found it, but you slid book by book. Pull the MIDDLE of what's left each time and the shelf halves with every check.

reveal.body: Each pull landed on the middle of the live shelf, so twelve became six became three. Halving what's left every check is where the speed comes from.
reveal.inTheWild: log₂(n) checks: a thousand books found in ten, a million in twenty.

insights.not-halving: Sliding spine by spine still finds the book — but that's a linear scan, n checks. Halving is the whole win: each pull cuts the remaining shelf in two, turning n into log n. Skip it and shelving in order gained you nothing.

trust-clues.title: Believe the Number
trust-clues.hook: Each pulled book's number rules out a whole side of the shelf (it greys out). Never re-check a greyed-out book.
trust-clues.winText: You trusted every call number and stayed on the live side of the shelf. That discipline is what keeps the search fast.

failText.ignored-feedback: You pulled a book the numbers had already ruled out. A greyed side can't hold the target — keep every check inside the live range.

reveal.body: Every call number greyed out a whole side of the shelf, and you only pulled from what stayed lit. Letting the number discard half is what keeps the shelf shrinking.
reveal.inTheWild: looking up a word: the page you land on tells you to flip left or right, never both.

insights.ignored-feedback: Because the shelf is sorted, a number above the target proves the target is entirely to the left — a certainty, not a guess. Re-checking a greyed book re-searches shelf you already ruled out; believing the number is what earns you the right to drop half.

boss-vault.title: BOSS · The Long Shelf
boss-vault.hook: Sixteen books. Four checks. Middle, then middle, then middle — a clean binary search or the reader walks.
boss-vault.winText: Sixteen books to one in four clean checks. A perfect binary search — the reader has their book.

failText.not-halving: Too many checks — you didn't halve. Sixteen books needs only four middle-pulls.
failText.ignored-feedback: You re-checked a greyed-out book. Trust every number — that's what makes four checks enough.

learn.frame: Both habits now, no prompts: halve the shelf AND believe every number — what do they add up to together?

reveal.body: Middle to split, the call number to choose the surviving half, repeat — sixteen books down to one in four checks. Halving guided by the number, all the way, IS binary search.
reveal.inTheWild: std::lower_bound, a database index seek, git bisect — each one halves and follows the comparison.

insights.not-halving: The halving is the speed: four checks only reach sixteen books if each one drops half the shelf. Slide instead of split and four pulls fall short.
insights.ignored-feedback: The number is what makes each cut valid — it proves which half to keep. Ignore it and you're halving blind, with no promise the book is even on the side you held onto.

narrow-drill.title: SIDE-QUEST · Middle Every Time
narrow-drill.hook: Drill one habit: after each check, go to the MIDDLE of whatever's still lit. Eight books, go.
narrow-drill.winText: Middle every time — you halved the shelf on every check. That habit IS binary search.

reveal.body: After each check you went straight to the middle of the lit shelf — nothing fancier. Middle, middle, middle until one spine is left is the engine under the whole thing.
reveal.inTheWild: the midpoint every loop: mid = (lo + hi) / 2.
